skill.name: delon-util
skill.description: @delon/util guidance for Angular utility functions, including array, string, date, number, and browser helpers.
skill.summary: Use @delon/util helpers for common Angular data formatting and state tasks.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 55 potential issues, but review found they are documentation false positives. The file contains Markdown TypeScript examples for @delon/util and no executable shell commands, credential material, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, network access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The scanner treated Markdown code fences and inline backtick text as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines document TypeScript imports, examples, Angular templates, and headings; they do not execute commands from the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged location is inside Markdown prose or a fenced TypeScript example. No shell invocation, subprocess API, or executable script file is present.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation is required. Marketplace indexing may tune command detectors to ignore Markdown code fences for documentation-only skills.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary documentation text and sort direction examples, including the front matter description and the string desc. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or crypto API use appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines describe utility functions or use desc as a descending sort option. There is no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive hashing context.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation is required. Keep cryptography detectors context-aware for words such as description and desc.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Template Key Text Flagged as Certificate Material
low_findings.description: The certificate and key alerts occur in an Angular template example that references group.key. This is a JavaScript object property, not a certificate, private key, or credential file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is an Angular loop over grouped task data. No PEM block, private key filename, certificate body, or secret value is present.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation is required. Sensitive-file detectors should require credential context before flagging key property names.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Application Methods Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts correspond to task form methods and performance guidance. They do not call host discovery commands, enumerate files, inspect processes, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are TypeScript application examples and plain text guidance. No command such as whoami, uname, env, ps, or network discovery appears there.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation is required. Reconnaissance detectors should ignore unrelated application method names in documentation.

content.value_statement: Angular teams often repeat common formatting, cloning, grouping, and date range logic. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical @delon/util patterns for ng-alain applications.
actual_capabilities: Explains @delon/util array helpers such as deepCopy, deepMerge, groupBy, uniqBy, and orderBy.
Shows string formatting and case conversion patterns for Angular applications.
Provides date range and relative time examples with getTimeDistance and formatDistanceToNow.
Documents number formatting helpers for currency, percentages, decimals, and thousands separators.
Demonstrates browser helpers such as clipboard copying, scrolling, deep property access, and equality checks.
Includes Angular component examples that use signals, computed values, and ng-zorro messages.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install @delon/util or modify project files by itself.
Examples focus on @delon/util version 20.1.0 and may need review for other versions.
It assumes Angular and ng-alain conventions, so some patterns may not fit other frameworks.
It does not replace project-specific validation, performance testing, or security review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Angular Utility Usage
use_cases.description: Adopt consistent helper functions for cloning, merging, grouping, and sorting application data.

use_cases.title: Build Task Management Interfaces
use_cases.description: Use practical examples for task lists, task links, date ranges, and formatted task metadata.

use_cases.title: Review Utility Choices During Migration
use_cases.description: Map repeated local helper code to @delon/util functions during ng-alain cleanup work.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right Utility
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to handle [task] in an Angular app. Which @delon/util helper should I use, and why?

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Local Helpers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Angular helper code and suggest equivalent @delon/util functions. Explain any behavior differences before changing it.

prompt_templates.title: Design Data Formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a utility plan for formatting task titles, dates, currency values, and grouped lists using @delon/util.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Utility Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component for expensive @delon/util calls. Recommend where to use computed values, caching, or simpler data structures.

output_examples.input: Ask for help grouping and sorting task records by status and priority.
output_examples.output: A concise recommendation to use groupBy for status buckets and orderBy for priority ordering, with notes about computed signals.

output_examples.input: Ask how to prepare task form data before editing and submitting.
output_examples.output: Guidance to deep copy original data, merge defaults, detect empty required fields, and compare form changes.

output_examples.input: Ask which utilities help display task dates and currency values.
output_examples.output: A short mapping from display needs to date and number helpers, including range, relative time, currency, and percentage formatting.

best_practices: Import specific @delon/util modules so application bundles stay easier to optimize.
Use deepCopy and deepMerge intentionally because large objects can make these operations expensive.
Wrap repeated grouping, sorting, and date calculations in computed values when using Angular signals.
anti_patterns: Do not mutate source state after cloning data for an edit or submit workflow.
Do not call expensive utility functions repeatedly from templates without caching or computed values.
Do not assume utility formatting handles every locale or business rule without product review.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding tools recommend and explain @delon/util helpers for Angular and ng-alain projects.

faq.question: Does it run code or shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide with TypeScript examples and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which @delon/util areas are covered?
faq.answer: It covers array, string, date, number, and browser utility examples.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this during refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes. They can use it as reference material when replacing local helpers or adding utility calls.

faq.question: Is it limited to task management examples?
faq.answer: No. Task management examples show usage patterns, but the utilities apply to many Angular workflows.

faq.question: What should users verify before applying suggestions?
faq.answer: Users should verify package version, import paths, edge cases, and performance impact in their own project.
